text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each time new code is added, a CI pipeline starts an automated build process. This means the system compiles the code and then runs a series of tests automatically. The main goal is to catch issues early, before they grow into bigger problems. If a test fails, the team is alerted immediately so they can fix it. By integrating regularly, developers avoid last-minute surprises and ensure the project remains stable. Overall, CI helps teams work more efficiently and deliver higher quality software.
